concerns.text: Threshold calibration claims 'without labeled data' (US3) but evaluation requires F1/precision/recall which need ground truth labels. If thresholds are tuned on labeled data to optimize these metrics, validation is circular—threshold selection depends on the same labels used for evaluation. Specify whether threshold calibration uses unsupervised criteria (e.g., score distribution percentiles) or acknowledge labeled data is used and restructure validation accordingly.

Paired t-test on F1-scores across 3-5 datasets is statistically underpowered for meaningful inference. With n=3-5, t-tests cannot reliably detect differences even with large effect sizes. Use non-parametric alternatives (Wilcoxon signed-rank) or report confidence intervals via bootstrap resampling instead.

Constitution Principle VI requires Bayesian inference convergence diagnostics (ESS for MCMC or ELBO convergence for ADVI). Spec only mentions 'ELBO convergence logs' without defining convergence criteria (e.g., ELBO delta threshold, iteration limits, or posterior stability checks). Models failing convergence must be excluded from evaluation.

Constitution Principle VII requires prior sensitivity analysis for Dirichlet process concentration parameters (alpha, gamma). Spec does not specify how concentration prior variations will be tested or what range of specifications constitutes 'reasonable' sensitivity analysis. This is a domain-standard requirement for Bayesian nonparametric claims.

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without defining the threshold. While data-model.md specifies minItems:1000, the spec should explicitly state '≥1000 observations' to enable unambiguous verification of statistical power for anomaly rate detection (1-5% injection rates require adequate sample sizes).
